overview: Smithery is a marketplace and registry for AI agents (specifically MCP - Model Context Protocol servers). It allows developers to publish MCP servers for distribution, analytics, and discovery. Servers are listed under namespaces and can be discovered by users via the registry API or web interface.

method_1_web_form.name: Web Form (URL-based)
method_1_web_form.description: Go to https://smithery.ai/new and enter your server's public HTTPS URL
steps: 2. Enter your MCP server's public HTTPS URL
3. Complete the publishing flow (likely involves authentication and metadata)
4. Smithery scans your server to extract metadata (tools, prompts, resources)

requirements.client_registration: Handled automatically via Client ID Metadata Documents

method_1_web_form.notes: If scanning fails due to auth walls, you can provide a static server card at `/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json` with manual metadata.

method_2_cli_url.name: CLI - URL Publishing
method_2_cli_url.description: Publish via CLI with a public URL
method_2_cli_url.notes: Can optionally pass --config-schema for configuration UI

method_3_cli_bundle.name: CLI - MCPB Bundle Publishing
method_3_cli_bundle.description: Publish a local stdio server via MCPB bundle
requirements: Prepare a .mcpb bundle (see Anthropic's MCPB guide)
Bundle is distributed to clients for local execution
method_3_cli_bundle.notes: MCPB bundles are pre-built artifacts clients can download and run locally

method_4_api.name: REST API
method_4_api.description: Programmatic publishing via Smithery Platform API

parameters.qualifiedName: The server's qualified name (e.g. 'namespace/server' or 'namespace'). Use %2F to encode the slash.

publish_server_release.description: Submit a release via multipart form. Supports hosted (JS module upload), external (URL), and stdio (MCPB bundle) release types.

multipart.module: binary (optional) - JavaScript module file (for hosted releases)
multipart.sourcemap: binary (optional) - Source map file (for hosted releases)
multipart.bundle: binary (optional) - MCPB bundle file (for stdio releases)

response.mcpUrl: string - The MCP endpoint URL for connecting

namespace.description: A namespace is required to publish servers. It groups your servers, connections, and skills under a shared identifier.
namespace.naming_rules: Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, globally unique

authentication.api: API key or Bearer token in Authorization header
authentication.token_scoping: Service tokens can be restricted by namespace, resource, operation, metadata, and TTL

server_metadata.description: Brief description of what the server does
server_metadata.icon: Server icon image (max 1MB, formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF, SVG, WebP)

release_artifacts.url_based: Public HTTPS URL with Streamable HTTP transport

verification_process.description: After publishing, servers can get verified by opening the server's Settings → Verification page

scanning_process.description: Smithery scans your server to extract metadata (tools, prompts, resources)
scanning_process.auth_servers: Prompted to authenticate so scan can complete

troubleshooting.403_forbidden: Ensure server returns 401 (not 403) for unauthenticated requests per MCP auth spec
troubleshooting.fallback: Use static server card at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json to bypass scanning

client_listing.description: Client developers can get their MCP clients listed on server pages
client_listing.attribution: Required - either Registry API integration or backlink to smithery.ai

additional_features.analytics: Track tool calls, monitor usage patterns
additional_features.oauth_ui: Automatic authentication modal for servers requiring config/API keys
additional_features.spec_compliance: Gateway handles MCP protocol compliance automatically
additional_features.deep_linking: Deep links for seamless integration into MCP clients
additional_features.uplink: Expose local MCP server as Smithery connection without deploying
